info.title: Service Health Historical Data APIs
info.description: APIs for requesting historical data recorded by the Crosswork Service Health application.

requestBody.description: The historicalServiceRequest parameter. Note: For L3VPN, if there are more than 50 endpoints, it's recommended to select the specific endpoints you need. The result historical timeline series will contain only the selected endpoints.

ruleId.description: The ID of the rule used by the service.

profileId.description: The ID of the profile used by the service.

monitoringType.description: Monitoring Type (Basic or Advanced) used by the service.

degradedPathsOnly.description: The response to use with services/subservices in a down or degraded state. See the AssuranceReportRequest API for details.

error.description: Error details in case the request failed.

rootSubservices.description: List of IDs of the subservice nodes that are directly connected to the service node.

moreFragments.description: Flag to indicate if more Kafka message chunking fragments are available

assuranceReportResponseStatus.description: Service assurance report is shared via NB using below schema.

checkHistoricalDataExistsResponse.description: Check if historical data exists for the given Service

archiveDataExists.description: True if historical data exists for the given Service

error.description: Error details in case the request failed.

configHandleMonitorError.description: Global settings configuration to handle the Monitor Error.

configHandleMonitorError.description: Global settings configuration to handle the Monitor Error.

DashboardServiceCount.description: Current count of services of a particular service type

DashboardSlaResponse.description: Response - SLA breaches count for SH Dashboard

serviceBreach.description: SLA sessions and breaches of the services

DashboardSlaResponseStatus.description: Response Status - SLA breaches count for SH Dashboard

ErrorMaintenance.description: Represents a single error occurring in the computation that is preventing its evaluation.

ErrorUninitialized.description: Presents a result error due to some initial unavailability of (sufficient) expression input data.

name.description: The name of this parameter. Examples: DEVICE, IFNAME, IFENTRY, VPN.

HPElemParamType.description: ParamType allows HPMgr to interpret the nature of the argument values passed while instantiating HP Elements like Rule, Subservice, etc.

HealthStatus.description:
Service Health Statuses: 
 - UNKNOWN: Default state displayed by CNC-ISTP, when monitoring is not enabled for a given service.
 - MONITORING_INITIATED: When a monitoring request is received for a service, Service Health initializes the service or subservice to this state.
 - MONITORING_FAILED: Indicates Service Health has encountered an internal error or failure.
 - MONITORING_PAUSED: Indicates that monitoring is currently paused, but historical data is still available for inspection through the timeline.
 - SERVICE_UP: At the service level, this state indicates that all the individual subservices of the monitored service are in good health and operating without any issues to report.
 - SERVICE_DOWN: All the different subservices of the Service that are being monitored are reporting issues. Corresponding symptoms should provide more details about the issues being encountered.
 - SERVICE_DEGRADED: At the Service level, this state indicates that some of the subservices of the Service that is being monitored are reporting issues. Corresponding symptoms should provide more details about the issues being encountered.

eventTimestamp.description: A timestamp indicating when an event of significance happened.

vpnNodeIds.description: Device Ids or Endpoints of the service

graphOnly.description: When set to true will return graph minimal data for consumers like UI to render VPN graph

serviceId.description: Request KPI Metrics data for the Service-Id

error.description: Error details in case the request failed

KpiMetricsResponseMetricTimestampValues.description: Timestamp and corresponding value of the metric.

metricLabel.description: Metric identifier. SubserviceClass definition provides this label value.

unitType.description: Unit type for the given metric

updateTimestamp.description: The time when the feed was last updated

eventTimestamp.description: The timestamp when the event of significance happened

error.description: Error details in case the request failed

linesData.description: Each element of the list represents a set of data points corresponding to a line in the metric chart.

MetricsResponseMetricTimestampValues.description: Timestamp and corresponding value of the metric.

MonitoringStatus.description:
Monitoring statuses: 
 - MONITORING_UNKNOWN: Default status, set when the monitoring status is unknown.
 - MONITORING_SUCCESS: Success state when there are no errors in monitoring.
 - ENCOUNTERED_ERRORS: State reflects the monitoring errors.

profileConstants.description:
map<key, value>
   key:    Constant name
   value:  Value defined for this constant in the respective Config Profile
Example:
  "profileConstants": {
       "CPU_THRESHOLD_MAX": "80"
  }

OutputSimpleValueStatus.description: Status which represents the health and symptoms.

totalSessions.description: Total probe sessions of the service

totalBreaches.description: Total session breaches of the service

softRootSubserviceNodes.description: List of Subservice-ids that have a soft relationship with the service node.

args.description: List of arguments passed to this subservice.

values.description: Given argument could be a list of values like [Tor-A, Tor-B] for given key-name 'Devices'

healthTs.description: The last time subservice health got updated.

trackerTs.description: The last time subservice health was updated due to a health update sent by a tracker.

SubserviceOperStateRuleTriggerConditions.description: Subservice Oper State Rule Trigger Conditions

ruleTriggerParamMap.description: The map to store the received conditional parameters with their values.

paramMapWithTimestamp.description: The map with the key as the parameter name and the value as the timestamp.

staticId.description: The combination of subServiceClass, namespace and config

dynamicSS.description: Flag indicating that the given subservice is dynamic.

subDeps.description: Hard Dependencies: List of subservice-ids which are of string type.

softSubDeps.description: Soft Dependencies: List of subservice-ids which are of string type.

dynamicSubDeps.description: List of hard and soft dynamic subservice dependencies. These lists can change dynamically independent of the rest of the Service Graph.

error.description: Error details in case the request failed.

eventTimestamp.description: The timestamp when the event of significance happened

message.description: Represents a single error occurring in the computation and preventing its evaluation

bearerAuth.description: Security token for authorizing requests for these APIs.
bearerAuth.name: Authorization
